title: Question 1
statement_text: Is it true that for every graph class $\mathcal{C}$ with polynomial expansion, there exists a polynomial function $f$ such that for every integer $r\geqslant 0$ and every graph $G\in\mathcal{C}$, $$\mathrm{scol}_{r}(G)\leqslant f(r)?$$
context_text: Esperet and Raymond asked whether polynomial expansion of a graph class implies polynomial strong $r$-coloring numbers. A positive answer would yield a new characterization of polynomial expansion in terms of polynomial strong coloring numbers. The present paper introduces four new bounded-radius parameters (depth-$r$ bramble number, tangle number, linkedness, well-linkedness) hoping they provide tools toward a positive answer.
paper_contribution: Introduces depth-$r$ bramble number, tangle number, linkedness, and well-linkedness, proves they are pairwise polynomially related and all polynomially bounded in $r$ for classes with polynomial expansion, providing potential new tools toward resolving the question positively.

title: Question 2
statement_text: Is it true that for every graph class $\mathcal{C}$ with polynomial expansion, there exists a polynomial function $f$ such that for every integer $r\geqslant 0$ and every graph $G\in\mathcal{C}$, $$\mathrm{scol}_{r}(G)\leqslant f(r,\mathrm{bn}_{r}(G))?$$
context_text: A positive answer to this question would be sufficient to answer Question 1 positively. The polynomial expansion assumption on $\mathcal{C}$ is essential: without it, $\mathrm{scol}_r(G)$ cannot be bounded by any function of $r$ and the depth-$r$ bramble number $\mathrm{bn}_r(G)$, even if the bramble number is replaced by $\mathrm{bn}_{g(r)}(G)$ for an arbitrary function $g$.

title: Question 13
statement_text: Is it true that for every graph class $\mathcal{C}$ with polynomial expansion, there exists a polynomial function $f$ such that for every integer $r\geqslant 0$ and every graph $G\in\mathcal{C}$, $$\mathrm{scol}_{r}(G)\leqslant f(r,\mathrm{grid}_{\mathrm{poly}(r)}(G))?$$
context_text: For $r=\infty$, grid minors are intimately related to $\mathrm{scol}_r$ via treewidth, but for finite $r$ this connection breaks down: for every $r,k\geqslant 1$ and every non-forest $H$, there exist graphs without $H$ as a depth-$k$ minor but with arbitrarily large strong $r$-coloring number. The question asks whether the connection survives when restricted to classes with polynomial expansion.
